It is up to the parties to manage their motions. Both parties have sought to seal portions of their filings on this motion, and both parties noticed their motion to seal for a date after this motion was to be heard. If the motion to seal is denied, the moving party has a choice to make, which may affect adjudication of the underlying motion. (See California Rules of Court, rule 2.551(b)(6).) Here, both parties have sealing motions, and neither party took any action to ensure their-let alone both-sealing motion(s) can be adjudicated consistent with the Rules of Court. The parties seem to believe management of their various motions in a way that makes practical and legal sense is up to the court. The court generally does not have the resources to do this on its own. In the future on such circumstances in this action the court may adjudicate the underlying motion and off calendar or deny as moot the sealing motion.

Prior to the hearing, Moving Defendants shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order repeating the above verbatim.

Order is signed.

Off Calendar Nov-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CAILIN HARDELL's Motion To Seal.

Plaintiff Cailin Hardell's unopposed Motion To Seal is GRANTED.

A court may order that a record be filed under seal only if it expressly finds facts that establish: (1) an overriding interest exists that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the records; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and, (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. (Cal. Rules of Ct., rule 2.550(d)(1)-(5); NBC Subsidiary (KNBC-TV), Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (Locke) (1999) 20 Cal.4th 1178, 1216-1218 ("NBC Subsidiary").) The party seeking the sealing of a record bears the burden of presenting information sufficient to identify the specific information claimed to be entitled to such treatment and the nature of the harm threatened by disclosure. (H.B. Fuller Co. v. Doe (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 879, 894.) The moving party must set forth specific facts in support of a motion to seal; statements that are too conclusory, abstract, or generic are not sufficient to satisfy the burden. (Id. at pp. 895-896.)

Good cause appearing, the motion to seal is granted. Exhibit 3 to the Declaration of Cailin Hardell shall be sealed in part as demonstrated in the motion, the redacted version of Exhibit 3 to the Declaration of Cailin Hardell shall remain in the public record and the unredacted version shall be sealed.

This tentative ruling is conditioned on Plaintiff's submission of a proposed order repeating verbatim the above text and adding text to comply with California Rules of Court, rule 2.550(d), and emailing it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
